{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_0a8258e9ebee47c09fe540cc","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# Cluster Configuration Norms\n2\t\n3\tThis directory contains Kubernetes manifests organized by cluster and namespace, deployed via ArgoCD GitOps.\n4\t\n5\t## Directory Structure\n6\t\n7\t```\n8\tk8s/\n9\t├── <cluster-name>/                    # One directory per cluster\n10\t│   ├── <cluster>-application.yml      # ArgoCD Application for entire cluster\n11\t│   ├── <cluster>-applicationset.yml   # ArgoCD ApplicationSet for dynamic apps\n12\t│   └── <namespace>/                   # One directory per namespace (FLAT - no subfolders)\n13\t│       ├── namespace.yml              # Namespace definition (required)\n14\t│       ├── deployment.yml             # Deployment manifest\n15\t│       ├── service.yml                # Service manifest\n16\t│       ├── configmap.yml              # ConfigMap\n17\t│       ├── <app>-sealedsecret.yml     # Encrypted secrets (safe for git)\n18\t│       ├── <app>-secret.yml.template  # Secret template with per-value instructions\n19\t│       └── ingressroute.yml           # Traefik IngressRoute\n20\t```\n21\t\n22\t**IMPORTANT:** Namespace directories are FLAT - no subdirectories allowed. All manifests for a namespace go directly in the namespace folder. Use filename prefixes to organize multiple apps (e.g., `myapp-deployment.yml`, `myapp-service.yml`).\n23\t\n24\t## Clusters\n25\t\n26\t| Cluster | Purpose | Server URL |\n27\t|---------|---------|------------|\n28\t| `apexalgo-iad` | Production workloads (IAD region) | `https://hcp-99476ebb-4133-4a21-ac6a-6e2bdf6794c0.spot.rackspace.com` |\n29\t| `ardenone-cluster` | Home lab / development | `https://k3s-server-a.ardenone.com:6443` |\n30\t| `ardenone-hub` | Hub cluster (Hetzner VPS, Liqo + Garage S3) | `https://ardenone-hub.ardenone.com:6443` |\n31\t| `ardenone-manager` | Management cluster (runs ArgoCD) | `https://kubernetes.default.svc` |\n32\t| `rs-manager` | Rackspace Spot management cluster (IAD); runs ArgoCD for iad-* clusters | `https://kubernetes.default.svc` (in-cluster) |\n33\t| `iad-acb` | ai-code-battle workloads (Rackspace Spot, IAD); managed by rs-manager ArgoCD | `https://hcp-ffb2da77-ad4e-4468-acfb-e1b3d477a8d7.spot.rackspace.com` |\n34\t| `iad-ci` | CI/CD builds (Rackspace Spot, IAD); managed by rs-manager ArgoCD | `https://hcp-de5bec10-ce14-4eed-a6f4-750f3fd3a89a.spot.rackspace.com` |\n35\t\n36\t## File Naming Conventions\n37\t\n38\t### Extensions\n39\t- Use `.yml` (preferred) or `.yaml` consistently within a namespace\n40\t- Both are valid, but prefer `.yml` for brevity\n41\t\n42\t### Common File Names\n43\t| File | Purpose |\n44\t|------|---------|\n45\t| `namespace.yml` | Namespace definition (REQUIRED for each namespace dir) |\n46\t| `deployment.yml` | Deployment manifest |\n47\t| `service.yml` | Service manifest |\n48\t| `configmap.yml` | ConfigMap with non-sensitive config |\n49\t| `sealedsecret.yml` | SealedSecret (encrypted, safe for git) |\n50\t| `<app>-secret.yml.template` | Template for creating secrets (placeholders with instructions) |\n51\t| `ingressroute.yml` | Traefik IngressRoute for external access |\n52\t| `servicemonitor.yml` | Prometheus ServiceMonitor |\n53\t| `hpa.yml` | HorizontalPodAutoscaler |\n54\t| `rbac.yml` | RBAC (Role, RoleBinding, ServiceAccount) |\n55\t| `application.yml` | ArgoCD Application for Helm charts or external sources |\n56\t\n57\t### Naming Patterns\n58\t- `<app>-deployment.yml` - App-specific deployment\n59\t- `<app>-sealedsecret.yml` - App-specific sealed secret\n60\t- `<app>-application.yml` - ArgoCD Application for external Helm chart **(CRITICAL - see below)**\n61\t- `*.disabled` suffix - Temporarily disabled (not applied)\n62\t\n63\t### CRITICAL: ArgoCD Application Naming\n64\t\n65\t**ArgoCD Application manifests MUST end with `-application.yml` or `-application.yaml`.**\n66\t\n67\tThe ArgoCD deployment uses a two-tier pattern:\n68\t1. **ApplicationSet** syncs namespace directories (excluding `*application.yml`)\n69\t2. **App-of-Apps** (`<cluster>-application.yml`) syncs only `*application.yml` files to ArgoCD\n70\t\n71\t```yaml\n72\t# ❌ WRONG - Will NOT be deployed to ArgoCD\n73\tcoder-helm.yml           # Not matched by app-of-apps pattern\n74\text-postgres-operator.yml\n75\t\n76\t# ✅ CORRECT - Matches *application.yml pattern\n77\tcoder-application.yml\n78\text-postgres-operator-application.yml\n79\tkeda-application.yml\n80\t```\n81\t\n82\t**Why this matters:**\n83\t- Files matching `*application.yml` are **excluded** from namespace sync (they don't deploy to target cluster)\n84\t- These files are **included** by the app-of-apps Application (they deploy to ArgoCD namespace)\n85\t- Incorrect naming means your Helm chart Application never gets created in ArgoCD\n86\t\n87\t## Critical Rules\n88\t\n89\t### 1. ALWAYS Set ImagePullSecrets for Private Images\n90\t\n91\t**All deployments using private Docker Hub images MUST include `imagePullSecrets`.**\n92\t\n93\t```yaml\n94\t# ✅ CORRECT - ImagePullSecret configured\n95\tapiVersion: apps/v1\n96\tkind: Deployment\n97\tspec:\n98\t  template:\n99\t    spec:\n100\t      imagePullSecrets:\n101\t      - name: docker-hub-registry\n102\t      containers:\n103\t      - name: app\n104\t        image: ronaldraygun/my-app:1.0.0\n105\t\n106\t# ❌ WRONG - No ImagePullSecret (will fail with ImagePullBackOff)\n107\tapiVersion: apps/v1\n108\tkind: Deployment\n109\tspec:\n110\t  template:\n111\t    spec:\n112\t      containers:\n113\t      - name: app\n114\t        image: ronaldraygun/my-app:1.0.0  # Authentication required!\n115\t```\n116\t\n117\t**Available secrets by namespace:**\n118\t- `devpod`: `docker-hub-registry`, `forgejo-container-registry`\n119\t- `mcp`: Check with `kubectl get secrets -n mcp | grep registry`\n120\t\n121\t**Why this is critical:**\n122\t- The cluster uses a registry proxy (`localhost:7439`) that requires authentication\n123\t- Without `imagePullSecrets`, private images fail with: `pull access denied, repository does not exist or may require authorization`\n124\t- Public images (e.g., `alpine`, `nginx`) work without secrets\n125\t\n126\t### 2. NO Subdirectories Below Namespace Level\n127\tNamespace directories must be FLAT. All manifests go directly in the namespace folder.\n128\t\n129\t```\n130\t# ❌ Wrong - subdirectory under namespace\n131\tk8s/apexalgo-iad/my-namespace/my-app/deployment.yml\n132\t\n133\t# ✅ Correct - flat structure with prefixed filenames\n134\tk8s/apexalgo-iad/my-namespace/my-app-deployment.yml\n135\tk8s/apexalgo-iad/my-namespace/my-app-service.yml\n136\tk8s/apexalgo-iad/my-namespace/other-app-deployment.yml\n137\t```\n138\t\n139\t### 3. NEVER Commit Plain Secrets\n140\t```yaml\n141\t# ❌ NEVER commit kind: Secret with real values\n142\tapiVersion: v1\n143\tkind: Secret\n144\tstringData:\n145\t  API_KEY: \"real-secret-value\"  # DANGEROUS\n146\t\n147\t# ✅ Use SealedSecret instead\n148\tapiVersion: bitnami.com/v1alpha1\n149\tkind: SealedSecret\n150\tspec:\n151\t  encryptedData:\n152\t    API_KEY: AgBy8w...  # Encrypted, safe for git\n153\t```\n154\t\n155\t#### Secret Template Format (`<app>-secret.yml.template`)\n156\t\n157\tEvery namespace that needs secrets MUST have a `<app>-secret.yml.template` with placeholder values. This is committed to git and serves as documentation for what secrets are required and how to obtain them.\n158\t\n159\t**Naming convention:** Always use the `<app>-secret.yml.template` suffix. Never use bare `secret.yml.template`.\n160\t\n161\tEach secret value MUST have an inline comment explaining how to find or generate it:\n162\t\n163\t```yaml\n164\t---\n165\t# My App Secret Template\n166\t# Fill in values, seal with kubeseal, then delete this file\n167\tapiVersion: v1\n168\tkind: Secret\n169\tmetadata:\n170\t  name: my-app-secret\n171\t  namespace: my-namespace\n172\ttype: Opaque\n173\tstringData:\n174\t  # API key from example.com — log in at https://example.com/account/api and copy your key\n175\t  API_KEY: \"REPLACE_ME\"\n176\t  # Generate with: openssl rand -base64 32\n177\t  DB_PASSWORD: \"REPLACE_ME\"\n178\t  # GitHub PAT with repo scope — https://github.com/settings/tokens\n179\t  GITHUB_TOKEN: \"REPLACE_ME\"\n180\t```\n181\t\n182\t#### Sealing Workflow\n183\t\n184\t```bash\n185\t# 1. Copy template and fill in real values (never commit this file)\n186\tcp my-app-secret.yml.template my-app-secret.yml\n187\tvi my-app-secret.yml  # fill in real values\n188\t\n189\t# 2. Seal with kubeseal (secrets are cluster-scoped — seal per cluster)\n190\tkubeseal --format yaml < my-app-secret.yml > my-app-sealedsecret.yml\n191\t\n192\t# 3. Delete the plain secret immediately\n193\trm my-app-secret.yml\n194\t\n195\t# 4. Commit only the sealed secret and template\n196\tgit add my-app-sealedsecret.yml my-app-secret.yml.template\n197\tgit commit -m \"feat(my-namespace): add sealed secret\"\n198\t```\n199\t\n200\t**Important:** SealedSecrets are cluster-specific. A secret sealed for `apexalgo-iad` cannot be decrypted on `ardenone-cluster` and vice versa. Always seal against the target cluster.\n201\t\n202\t### 4. NEVER Use Job or CronJob — Use Deployments with Internal Scheduling\n203\t\n204\t`kind: Job` and `kind: CronJob` are **permanently banned** from this repo. This is not a preference — it is a hard rule enforced by code review.\n205\t\n206\t**Why:**\n207\t- ArgoCD cannot manage Jobs properly (non-idempotent: re-sync re-creates them)\n208\t- CronJob-spawned Job pods are **not owned by ArgoCD** — they are never pruned when the CronJob manifest is removed or disabled. Orphaned pods hold CPU and memory reservations indefinitely, silently starving other workloads. This has caused real scheduling failures in this cluster.\n209\t- `ImagePullBackOff` Job pods have 0 restarts and never hit `backoffLimit`, so they sit forever consuming scheduler resources with no automatic cleanup.\n210\t\n211\t**Use a Deployment with an internal scheduling loop instead:**\n212\t\n213\t```yaml\n214\t# ❌ NEVER — CronJob creates orphaned Job pods ArgoCD cannot prune\n215\tapiVersion: batch/v1\n216\tkind: CronJob\n217\tmetadata:\n218\t  name: my-task\n219\tspec:\n220\t  schedule: \"0 * * * *\"\n221\t  jobTemplate: ...\n222\t\n223\t# ❌ NEVER — same orphan problem, plus ArgoCD re-creates on every sync\n224\tapiVersion: batch/v1\n225\tkind: Job\n226\tmetadata:\n227\t  name: my-task\n228\t\n229\t# ✅ CORRECT — long-running Deployment with internal sleep/schedule loop\n230\tapiVersion: apps/v1\n231\tkind: Deployment\n232\tmetadata:\n233\t  name: my-task\n234\tspec:\n235\t  replicas: 1\n236\t  template:\n237\t    spec:\n238\t      containers:\n239\t      - name: my-task\n240\t        command:\n241\t          - sh\n242\t          - -c\n243\t          - |\n244\t            while true; do\n245\t              run-my-task\n246\t              sleep 3600   # run hourly\n247\t            done\n248\t```\n249\t\n250\tFor more sophisticated scheduling (cron expressions, skip-if-still-running logic), implement it inside the application process itself (e.g., Python `schedule` library, Go `robfig/cron`, Rust `tokio-cron-scheduler`). The container stays running; the work happens on an internal timer.\n251\t\n252\t**Existing violations in this cluster:**\n253\t- `devimprint/newsletter-generator` — CronJob left active after manifest was `.disabled`'d; its Job pod has been in ImagePullBackOff for weeks holding 250m CPU on a node. Must be manually deleted via `kubectl delete job -n devimprint --selector=job-name=...` with cluster write access.\n254\t\n255\t### 5. Use Traefik IngressRoute (not Ingress)\n256\t```yaml\n257\t# ❌ Don't use standard Ingress\n258\tapiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1\n259\tkind: Ingress\n260\t\n261\t# ✅ Use Traefik IngressRoute\n262\tapiVersion: traefik.io/v1alpha1\n263\tkind: IngressRoute\n264\tspec:\n265\t  entryPoints:\n266\t    - websecure\n267\t  routes:\n268\t    - match: Host(`myapp.ardenone.com`)\n269\t      kind: Rule\n270\t      services:\n271\t        - name: myapp\n272\t          port: 8080\n273\t  tls:\n274\t    certResolver: letsencrypt\n275\t```\n276\t\n277\t### 6. Domain Naming (Cloudflare Limitation)\n278\tCloudflare doesn't support nested subdomains. Use hyphens:\n279\t\n280\t```yaml\n281\t# ❌ Wrong - nested subdomain\n282\tHost(`api.staging.ardenone.com`)\n283\t\n284\t# ✅ Correct - hyphenated\n285\tHost(`api-staging.ardenone.com`)\n286\t```\n287\t\n288\t### 7. Namespace Must Exist\n289\tEvery namespace directory MUST contain a `namespace.yml`:\n290\t\n291\t```yaml\n292\tapiVersion: v1\n293\tkind: Namespace\n294\tmetadata:\n295\t  name: my-namespace\n296\t  labels:\n297\t    app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: argocd\n298\t```\n299\t\n300\t### 8. Use Semver Tags for `ronaldraygun/*` Images — Never `:latest`, Never Bare SHAs\n301\t\n302\tAll deployments referencing `ronaldraygun/*` images MUST pin to a **semver tag** (`MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH`). `:latest` and bare git SHAs are both forbidden in manifests.\n303\t\n304\t```yaml\n305\t# ❌ WRONG — `:latest` silently changes what runs, breaks rollback\n306\timage: ronaldraygun/acb-api:latest\n307\t\n308\t# ❌ WRONG — SHA tags are not human-readable, don't communicate version intent,\n309\t#            and make it impossible to tell at a glance if a cluster is up-to-date\n310\timage: ronaldraygun/acb-api:sha-80334c6\n311\timage: ronaldraygun/acb-api:a144cf75\n312\t\n313\t# ✅ CORRECT — semver: readable, rollback-friendly, conveys change magnitude\n314\timage: ronaldraygun/acb-api:1.4.2\n315\timage: ronaldraygun/nap-detector:0.1.6\n316\t```\n317\t\n318\t**How to implement in CI WorkflowTemplates:**\n319\t\n320\tEvery container repo MUST have a `containers/<name>/VERSION` file containing the current semver. The CI template reads this file, pushes both `:<semver>` and `:latest`, and the manifest references the semver tag.\n321\t\n322\t```yaml\n323\t# ✅ Correct CI template pattern — reads VERSION file\n324\t- name: resolve-version\n325\t  script:\n326\t    command: [sh]\n327\t    source: |\n328\t      VERSION=$(cat containers/my-app/VERSION)\n329\t      echo \"$VERSION\" > /tmp/version\n330\t- name: build-container\n331\t  inputs:\n332\t    parameters:\n333\t    - name: version  # passed from resolve-version step\n334\t  container:\n335\t    args:\n336\t      - --destination=docker.io/ronaldraygun/my-app:{{inputs.parameters.version}}\n337\t      - --destination=docker.io/ronaldraygun/my-app:latest\n338\t```\n339\t\n340\t```yaml\n341\t# ❌ Wrong CI template pattern — SHA tag in annotation, no VERSION file\n342\tmetadata:\n343\t  annotations:\n344\t    commit_sha: abc123\n345\targs:\n346\t  - --destination=docker.io/ronaldraygun/my-app:{{workflow.annotations.commit_sha}}\n347\t```\n348\t\n349\t**Rules:**\n350\t- Bump the `VERSION` file as part of the same commit that changes the code.\n351\t- Use `PATCH` bumps for fixes, `MINOR` for new features, `MAJOR` for breaking changes.\n352\t- The manifest in declarative-config is updated to the new semver after a successful build.\n353\t- Public images (`alpine`, `nginx`, `postgres`) should also use pinned versions, not `:latest`.\n354\t\n355\t## Helm Application Conventions\n356\t\n357\t### Release Naming\n358\tArgoCD Application names determine the Helm release name. All resources are prefixed with `<chart>-<cluster>`:\n359\t\n360\t```yaml\n361\t# Application name: cert-manager-ardenone-cluster\n362\t# Creates: cert-manager-ardenone-cluster, cert-manager-ardenone-cluster-webhook, etc.\n363\t```\n364\t\n365\t**NEVER use `fullnameOverride` or `nameOverride`** — these break the naming convention and create duplicate resources with TLS mismatches.\n366\t\n367\t### Parameters vs valuesObject\n368\t- **`parameters`** for simple top-level values: `installCRDs`, `create-namespace`\n369\t- **`valuesObject`** for nested config: resources, replicas, webhook settings\n370\t\n371\t```yaml\n372\thelm:\n373\t  parameters:\n374\t    - name: installCRDs\n375\t      value: \"true\"\n376\t  valuesObject:\n377\t    resources:\n378\t      requests:\n379\t        cpu: 50m\n380\t        memory: 64Mi\n381\t```\n382\t\n383\t### Standard syncPolicy\n384\tAll Applications should use this baseline:\n385\t\n386\t```yaml\n387\tsyncPolicy:\n388\t  automated:\n389\t    prune: true\n390\t    selfHeal: true\n391\t    allowEmpty: true\n392\t  syncOptions:\n393\t    - Validate=true\n394\t    - CreateNamespace=true\n395\t    - PrunePropagationPolicy=foreground\n396\t    - PruneLast=true\n397\t    - RespectIgnoreDifferences=true\n398\t    - ServerSideApply=true\n399\t  retry:\n400\t    limit: 5\n401\t    backoff:\n402\t      duration: 5s\n403\t      factor: 2\n404\t      maxDuration: 3m\n405\trevisionHistoryLimit: 10\n406\t```\n407\t\n408\t### ignoreDifferences for Tailscale-managed Services\n409\tServices modified by the Tailscale operator need ignoreDifferences to prevent sync conflicts:\n410\t\n411\t```yaml\n412\tignoreDifferences:\n413\t  - group: \"\"\n414\t    kind: Service\n415\t    jsonPointers:\n416\t      - /spec/type\n417\t      - /spec/externalName\n418\t      - /metadata/finalizers\n419\t      - /status\n420\t```\n421\t\n422\t## Cross-Cluster Service Exposure\n423\t\n424\tServices are made accessible across clusters via the **Tailscale operator**. Each cluster runs the Tailscale operator which manages proxy pods for egress (reaching external services) and ingress (exposing cluster services to the tailnet).\n425\t\n426\t### Exposing a cluster service to the tailnet\n427\t\n428\tAdd the `tailscale.com/expose` annotation to a Service. The Tailscale operator creates a proxy pod that makes the service reachable on the tailnet.\n429\t\n430\t```yaml\n431\tapiVersion: v1\n432\tkind: Service\n433\tmetadata:\n434\t  name: my-service\n435\t  namespace: my-namespace\n436\t  annotations:\n437\t    tailscale.com/expose: \"true\"\n438\t    tailscale.com/hostname: my-service-cluster-name\n439\tspec:\n440\t  type: ClusterIP\n441\t  selector:\n442\t    app: my-service\n443\t  ports:\n444\t    - port: 80\n445\t      targetPort: 8080\n446\t```\n447\t\n448\tThe service becomes reachable at `my-service-cluster-name.tail1b1987.ts.net` from any node on the tailnet.\n449\t\n450\t### Reaching an external service from within a cluster (egress)\n451\t\n452\tCreate a selector-less Service with `tailscale.com/tailnet-fqdn` pointing to the target's Tailscale hostname. The operator creates a proxy pod that routes cluster traffic to the tailnet.\n453\t\n454\t```yaml\n455\tapiVersion: v1\n456\tkind: Service\n457\tmetadata:\n458\t  name: remote-service\n459\t  namespace: my-namespace\n460\t  annotations:\n461\t    tailscale.com/tailnet-fqdn: target-hostname.tail1b1987.ts.net\n462\tspec:\n463\t  type: ClusterIP\n464\t  ports:\n465\t    - port: 8200\n466\t      targetPort: 8200\n467\t```\n468\t\n469\tPods in the cluster reach the remote service at `remote-service.my-namespace.svc.cluster.local:8200`.\n470\t\n471\t**Example — OpenBao egress (used by apexalgo-iad and iad-ci):**\n472\t```yaml\n473\tannotations:\n474\t  tailscale.com/tailnet-fqdn: traefik-ardenone-hub.tail1b1987.ts.net\n475\t```\n476\tOpenBao is routed through Traefik's `openbao-api` TCP entrypoint (port 8200), not exposed directly to the tailnet.\n477\t\n478\t### Exposing services via Traefik vpn entrypoint\n479\t\n480\tFor HTTP/HTTPS services, prefer Traefik IngressRoutes on the `vpn` entrypoint over direct Tailscale exposure. The Tailscale operator already exposes Traefik itself to the tailnet, so additional services routed through Traefik don't need their own Tailscale proxy.\n481\t\n482\t```yaml\n483\tapiVersion: traefik.io/v1alpha1\n484\tkind: IngressRoute\n485\tspec:\n486\t  entryPoints:\n487\t    - vpn\n488\t  routes:\n489\t    - match: Host(`my-service.ardenone.com`)\n490\t      kind: Rule\n491\t      services:\n492\t        - name: my-service\n493\t          port: 80\n494\t  tls:\n495\t    secretName: vpn-wildcard-tls\n496\t```\n497\t\n498\t**When to use which:**\n499\t| Method | Use case |\n500\t|--------|----------|\n501\t| Traefik `vpn` entrypoint | HTTP/HTTPS services already behind Traefik — avoids extra Tailscale proxies |\n502\t| `tailscale.com/expose` | Non-HTTP services (TCP, gRPC) or services that need their own tailnet hostname |\n503\t| `tailscale.com/tailnet-fqdn` | Reaching a service on another cluster or external tailnet node from within a cluster |\n504\t\n505\t### ArgoCD Read-Only API\n506\t\n507\tA read-only ArgoCD API proxy is available on ardenone-manager's vpn entrypoint:\n508\t\n509\t```bash\n510\tcurl -sk https://argocd-ro-ardenone-manager-ts.ardenone.com:8444/api/v1/applications\n511\t```\n512\t\n513\t- No authentication required — proxy injects a read-only bearer token\n514\t- Tailscale vpn entrypoint only (not public internet)\n515\t- Read-only: applications, projects, clusters, repositories, logs\n516\t\n517\t## Common Annotations\n518\t\n519\t### Reloader (Stakater) — Auto-restart on Secret/ConfigMap Changes\n520\t```yaml\n521\t# Watch all referenced secrets/configmaps automatically\n522\tannotations:\n523\t  reloader.stakater.com/auto: \"true\"\n524\t\n525\t# Or explicitly name secrets to watch\n526\tannotations:\n527\t  secret.reloader.stakater.com/reload: \"my-secret,other-secret\"\n528\t```\n529\t\n530\t### Homepage (gethomepage.dev) — Service Discovery Dashboard\n531\t```yaml\n532\tannotations:\n533\t  gethomepage.dev/enabled: \"true\"\n534\t  gethomepage.dev/group: \"Category Name\"\n535\t  gethomepage.dev/name: \"App Name\"\n536\t  gethomepage.dev/description: \"Short description\"\n537\t  gethomepage.dev/pod-selector: \"app=my-app\"\n538\t  gethomepage.dev/icon: \"mdi-icon-name\"  # or URL\n539\t  gethomepage.dev/href: \"https://myapp.ardenone.com\"\n540\t```\n541\t\n542\t### External-DNS — Cloudflare Tunnel Routing\n543\t```yaml\n544\tannotations:\n545\t  external-dns.alpha.kubernetes.io/hostname: myapp.ardenone.com\n546\t  external-dns.alpha.kubernetes.io/target: <tunnel-id>.cfargotunnel.com\n547\t```\n548\t\n549\t### ArgoCD Image Updater — Automated Image Updates\n550\t```yaml\n551\tannotations:\n552\t  argocd-image-updater.argoproj.io/image-list: app=registry/image\n553\t  argocd-image-updater.argoproj.io/app.update-strategy: semver\n554\t  argocd-image-updater.argoproj.io/app.allow-tags: \"regexp:^(\\\\d+)\\\\.(\\\\d+)\\\\.(\\\\d+)$\"\n555\t  argocd-image-updater.argoproj.io/write-back-method: argocd\n556\t```\n557\t\n558\t## IngressRoute Patterns\n559\t\n560\t### Entrypoints\n561\t\n562\tTraefik has two ingress paths, each with its own entrypoint:\n563\t\n564\t| Entrypoint | Port | Source | Protocol | Use Case |\n565\t|------------|------|--------|----------|----------|\n566\t| `websecure` | 8443 | Cloudflare Tunnel (cloudflared) | HTTPS | Public internet access |\n567\t| `vpn` | 8444 | Headscale mesh (tailscale) | HTTPS | Internal VPN-only access |\n568\t\n569\tBoth entrypoints use TLS. VPN-only services still need a TLS certificate (or use Traefik's default cert).\n570\t\n571\t**Choose entrypoints based on visibility:**\n572\t\n573\t```yaml\n574\t# External only — accessible via Cloudflare (public internet)\n575\tentryPoints: [websecure]\n576\t\n577\t# Internal only — accessible via headscale VPN mesh\n578\tentryPoints: [vpn]\n579\t\n580\t# Both — accessible from internet AND VPN\n581\tentryPoints: [websecure, vpn]\n582\t```\n583\t\n584\t**Existing IngressRoutes default to `websecure` (external only).** Only add `vpn` when the service should be reachable from the headscale mesh.\n585\t\n586\t**Guidelines:**\n587\t- VPN-only services (`entryPoints: [vpn]`) do NOT need external-dns annotations\n588\t- VPN-only services MUST still create a Certificate resource and reference it via `tls.secretName`\n589\t- VPN-only services may skip the forward-auth middleware since headscale ACLs already control access\n590\t- Services exposed on both entrypoints can share the same TLS certificate\n591\t\n592\t**Example — VPN-only service:**\n593\t```yaml\n594\tapiVersion: cert-manager.io/v1\n595\tkind: Certificate\n596\tmetadata:\n597\t  name: my-internal-service-cert\n598\t  namespace: my-namespace\n599\tspec:\n600\t  secretName: my-internal-service-tls\n601\t  duration: 2160h\n602\t  renewBefore: 360h\n603\t  issuerRef:\n604\t    name: letsencrypt-cloudflare\n605\t    kind: ClusterIssuer\n606\t  commonName: \"my-internal-service.ardenone.com\"\n607\t  dnsNames:\n608\t    - \"my-internal-service.ardenone.com\"\n609\t---\n610\tapiVersion: traefik.io/v1alpha1\n611\tkind: IngressRoute\n612\tmetadata:\n613\t  name: my-internal-service\n614\t  namespace: my-namespace\n615\tspec:\n616\t  entryPoints:\n617\t    - vpn\n618\t  routes:\n619\t    - match: Host(`my-internal-service.ardenone.com`)\n620\t      kind: Rule\n621\t      services:\n622\t        - name: my-internal-service\n623\t          port: 8080\n624\t  tls:\n625\t    secretName: my-internal-service-tls\n626\t```\n627\t\n628\t**Example — dual access (internet + VPN):**\n629\t```yaml\n630\tapiVersion: traefik.io/v1alpha1\n631\tkind: IngressRoute\n632\tmetadata:\n633\t  name: my-service\n634\t  namespace: my-namespace\n635\t  annotations:\n636\t    external-dns.alpha.kubernetes.io/hostname: my-service.ardenone.com\n637\t    external-dns.alpha.kubernetes.io/target: <tunnel-id>.cfargotunnel.com\n638\tspec:\n639\t  entryPoints:\n640\t    - websecure\n641\t    - vpn\n642\t  routes:\n643\t    - match: Host(`my-service.ardenone.com`)\n644\t      kind: Rule\n645\t      services:\n646\t        - name: my-service\n647\t          port: 8080\n648\t      middlewares:\n649\t        - name: ardenone-com-traefik-auth\n650\t          namespace: traefik\n651\t  tls:\n652\t    secretName: my-service-tls\n653\t```\n654\t\n655\t### Authentication Middleware\n656\tMost public IngressRoutes reference a shared auth middleware in the traefik namespace:\n657\t\n658\t```yaml\n659\tmiddlewares:\n660\t  - name: ardenone-com-traefik-auth\n661\t    namespace: traefik\n662\t```\n663\t\n664\t### Certificate Issuers\n665\t- **`letsencrypt-cloudflare`** (ClusterIssuer) — for public domains via Cloudflare DNS challenge\n666\t- **`ardenone-ca-issuer`** (ClusterIssuer) — for internal/cluster-only TLS (e.g., webhooks, internal services)\n667\t\n668\tStandard certificate duration: 2160h (90d), renewBefore: 360h (15d).\n669\t\n670\t### TLS Certificates Are Required\n671\t\n672\tEvery IngressRoute on both entrypoints (`websecure` and `vpn`) MUST have a per-service Certificate resource and reference it via `tls.secretName`. There is no default/wildcard fallback. Do NOT use bare `tls: {}`.\n673\t\n674\t```yaml\n675\t# ✅ Correct — explicit cert\n676\ttls:\n677\t  secretName: my-service-tls\n678\t\n679\t# ❌ Wrong — no default cert configured\n680\ttls: {}\n681\t```\n682\t\n683\t## Disabled Files\n684\t\n685\tAppend `.disabled` to temporarily disable a manifest without deleting it:\n686\t```\n687\tmy-feature-deployment.yml.disabled    # Not applied by ArgoCD\n688\t```\n689\t\n690\t## ArgoCD Integration\n691\t\n692\t### ApplicationSet (Recommended)\n693\tEach cluster has an ApplicationSet that auto-discovers namespaces. It uses a two-tier pattern:\n694\t\n695\t1. **Manifest ApplicationSet** — syncs namespace directories, excludes `*application.yml`:\n696\t   - Include: `{*.yaml,*.yml}`\n697\t   - Exclude: `{ignore/*,*application.yml}`\n698\t   - Auto-names apps as `{{path.basename}}-ns-<cluster>` (e.g., `devpod-ns-ardenone-cluster`)\n699\t\n700\t2. **App-of-Apps Application** — syncs only `*application.yml` files to ArgoCD:\n701\t   - Include: `{*application.yaml,*application.yml}`\n702\t   - These deploy Helm charts and external sources\n703\t\n704\t```yaml\n705\t# apexalgo-iad-applicationset.yml\n706\tapiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1\n707\tkind: ApplicationSet\n708\tspec:\n709\t  generators:\n710\t  - git:\n711\t      repoURL: https://github.com/jedarden/declarative-config.git\n712\t      directories:\n713\t      - path: k8s/apexalgo-iad/*\n714\t```\n715\t\n716\t### Helm Chart Applications (`*application.yml`)\n717\t\n718\tFor Helm charts or external sources, create a file matching `*application.yml` in the cluster directory.\n719\t**These are automatically deployed — no `kubectl apply` needed.** The cluster's app-of-apps Application\n720\t(also in declarative-config) scans `k8s/<cluster>/` for `*application.yml` files and applies them to\n721\tArgoCD on push. Just push to `main`.\n722\t\n723\t**Management chain by cluster:**\n724\t- `k8s/ardenone-manager/*application.yml` → argocd on ardenone-manager (self-managing)\n725\t- `k8s/rs-manager/*application.yml` → `applications-rs-manager` app on rs-manager (self-managing)\n726\t- `k8s/iad-acb/*application.yml` → `applications-iad-acb` app on rs-manager (manages iad-acb)\n727\t- `k8s/iad-ci/*application.yml` → rs-manager ArgoCD (manages iad-ci)\n728\t\n729\t```yaml\n730\tapiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1\n731\tkind: Application\n732\tmetadata:\n733\t  name: my-helm-app-<cluster>\n734\t  namespace: argocd\n735\tspec:\n736\t  source:\n737\t    repoURL: https://charts.example.com\n738\t    chart: my-chart\n739\t    targetRevision: 1.0.0\n740\t  destination:\n741\t    server: https://<cluster-api-server>\n742\t    namespace: my-namespace\n743\t```\n744\t\n745\t## Adding a New Workload\n746\t\n747\t1. **Create namespace directory:**\n748\t   ```bash\n749\t   mkdir -p k8s/<cluster>/<namespace>\n750\t   ```\n751\t\n752\t2. **Add namespace.yml:**\n753\t   ```yaml\n754\t   apiVersion: v1\n755\t   kind: Namespace\n756\t   metadata:\n757\t     name: <namespace>\n758\t   ```\n759\t\n760\t3. **Add deployment and service:**\n761\t   ```bash\n762\t   # deployment.yml, service.yml, configmap.yml as needed\n763\t   ```\n764\t\n765\t4. **Add secrets (if needed):**\n766\t   ```bash\n767\t   # Create template\n768\t   cp secret.yml.template <app>-secret.yml\n769\t   # Fill in values, seal, delete original\n770\t   kubeseal --format yaml < <app>-secret.yml > <app>-sealedsecret.yml\n771\t   rm <app>-secret.yml\n772\t   ```\n773\t\n774\t5. **Commit and push:**\n775\t   ```bash\n776\t   git add k8s/<cluster>/<namespace>/\n777\t   git commit -m \"feat(<namespace>): add deployment manifests\"\n778\t   git push\n779\t   ```\n780\t\n781\t6. **ArgoCD auto-syncs** - ApplicationSet picks up new namespace\n782\t\n783\t7. **Monitor the sync** - After pushing, verify ArgoCD syncs successfully:\n784\t   ```bash\n785\t   # Via ArgoCD API (read-only token)\n786\t   curl -sk \"https://argocd-manager.ardenone.com/api/v1/applications/<app-name>\" \\\n787\t     -H \"Authorization: Bearer $ARGOCD_TOKEN\" | python3 -c \"\n788\t   import json, sys; data = json.load(sys.stdin)\n789\t   s = data['status']\n790\t   print(f'sync={s[\\\"sync\\\"][\\\"status\\\"]} health={s[\\\"health\\\"][\\\"status\\\"]}')\n791\t   for c in s.get('conditions', []):\n792\t       print(f'  {c[\\\"type\\\"]}: {c[\\\"message\\\"][:120]}')\n793\t   \"\n794\t\n795\t   # Via kubectl (if proxy is available)\n796\t   kubectl --server=http://kubectl-ardenone-cluster-1:8001 get pods -n <namespace>\n797\t   ```\n798\t   Watch for: CRD validation errors, webhook TLS mismatches, duplicate resource conflicts, and deployment progress deadline failures. Do not consider the task complete until the ArgoCD Application shows **Synced** and **Healthy**.\n799\t\n800\t## Debugging\n801\t\n802\t```bash\n803\t# Check ArgoCD sync status\n804\tkubectl get applications -n argocd | grep <namespace>\n805\t\n806\t# View sync details\n807\tkubectl describe application <app-name> -n argocd\n808\t\n809\t# Force sync\n810\targocd app sync <app-name>\n811\t\n812\t# Check pod status\n813\tkubectl get pods -n <namespace>\n814\tkubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n <namespace>\n815\tkubectl logs <pod-name> -n <namespace>\n816\t```\n817\t\n818\t## CI/CD — Checking Argo Workflow Logs\n819\t\n820\tAll CI runs on Argo Workflows in `iad-ci`. Workflow templates live in `k8s/iad-ci/argo-workflows/`.\n821\t\n822\t```bash\n823\t# List recent workflow runs and their status\n824\tkubectl --kubeconfig=/home/coding/.kube/iad-ci.kubeconfig \\\n825\t  get workflows -n argo-workflows --sort-by=.metadata.creationTimestamp | tail -20\n826\t\n827\t# Get a specific workflow's result and error message\n828\tkubectl --kubeconfig=/home/coding/.kube/iad-ci.kubeconfig \\\n829\t  get workflow <workflow-name> -n argo-workflows \\\n830\t  -o jsonpath='{.status.phase} - {.status.message}'\n831\t\n832\t# Get per-step failure details (most useful for debugging)\n833\tkubectl --kubeconfig=/home/coding/.kube/iad-ci.kubeconfig \\\n834\t  get workflow <workflow-name> -n argo-workflows -o json | python3 -c \"\n835\timport json,sys\n836\tw = json.load(sys.stdin)\n837\tfor node in w['status'].get('nodes',{}).values():\n838\t    if node.get('phase') in ('Failed','Error'):\n839\t        print(node['displayName'], '-', node['phase'])\n840\t        print('  msg:', node.get('message',''))\n841\t        for p in node.get('inputs',{}).get('parameters',[]):\n842\t            print(f'  input {p[\\\"name\\\"]}={p.get(\\\"value\\\")}')\n843\t\"\n844\t\n845\t# Stream logs from a running step pod\n846\tkubectl --kubeconfig=/home/coding/.kube/iad-ci.kubeconfig \\\n847\t  get pods -n argo-workflows -l workflows.argoproj.io/workflow=<workflow-name>\n848\tkubectl --kubeconfig=/home/coding/.kube/iad-ci.kubeconfig \\\n849\t  logs -n argo-workflows <pod-name> -c main -f\n850\t```\n851\t\n852\t> **Critical:** `podGC: OnPodCompletion` deletes pods immediately after they finish — you cannot retrieve logs after the fact via kubectl. Either stream logs while the pod is running, or use the Argo UI at `https://argo-ci.ardenone.com` (logs are kept for 30min on success, 2h on failure).\n853\t\n854\t### Submitting a build manually\n855\t\n856\t```bash\n857\tkubectl --kubeconfig=/home/coding/.kube/iad-ci.kubeconfig create -f - <<EOF\n858\tapiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1\n859\tkind: Workflow\n860\tmetadata:\n861\t  generateName: <template-name>-manual-\n862\t  namespace: argo-workflows\n863\tspec:\n864\t  workflowTemplateRef:\n865\t    name: <template-name>\n866\tEOF\n867\t```\n868\t\n869\t## Rackspace Spot Storage Classes\n870\t\n871\tAll Rackspace Spot clusters (iad-ci, iad-native-ads, iad-options, iad-kalshi) use OpenStack Cinder CSI. The correct storage classes are:\n872\t\n873\t| Class | Use case |\n874\t|-------|----------|\n875\t| `sata` | Default — general workloads, databases, queues |\n876\t| `sata-large` | Large volumes (>100Gi) |\n877\t| `ssd` | **Do not use** — reserved/default on some clusters but avoid |\n878\t| `ssd-large` | **Do not use** |\n879\t\n880\t**Always use `sata` or `sata-large` on Rackspace Spot clusters.** Never use `ssd`, `local-path`, or omit `storageClassName` (the default varies by cluster).\n881\t\n882\t## Rackspace Spot Node Pools\n883\t\n884\tNode pools are configured in the Rackspace Spot UI — they are **not** managed through declarative-config.\n885\t\n886\t**Always use spot node pools. Never create or keep on-demand node pools.** On-demand nodes on Rackspace Spot cost significantly more than spot and defeat the purpose of using Spot clusters. If an on-demand node pool exists, delete it from the Spot UI immediately.\n887\t\n888\tTo identify node types on a running cluster:\n889\t```bash\n890\tkubectl get nodes -o json | python3 -c \"\n891\timport json,sys\n892\tfor n in json.load(sys.stdin)['items']:\n893\t    name = n['metadata']['name']\n894\t    ntype = n['metadata']['labels'].get('servers.ngpc.rxt.io/type', '?')\n895\t    pool = n['metadata']['labels'].get('nodepool.ngpc.rxt.io/name', '?')\n896\t    print(f'{name}: type={ntype} pool={pool}')\n897\t\"\n898\t```\n899\t\n900\tAny node showing `type=ondemand` must be investigated — its node pool should be deleted from the Spot UI.\n901\t\n902\t## Common Mistakes\n903\t\n904\t| Mistake | Fix |\n905\t|---------|-----|\n906\t| Subdirectory under namespace | Flatten - use filename prefixes instead |\n907\t| Committing plain Secret | Use SealedSecret |\n908\t| Using `kind: Job` or `kind: CronJob` | **Hard ban** — use Deployment with internal scheduling loop; see Rule #4 |\n909\t| Missing namespace.yml | Add namespace definition |\n910\t| Nested subdomain | Use hyphenated subdomain |\n911\t| Using Ingress | Use Traefik IngressRoute |\n912\t| Using `:latest` for `ronaldraygun/*` images | Pin to an immutable SHA or semver tag |\n913\t| Missing resource limits | Add requests/limits |\n914\t| ArgoCD App named `*-helm.yml` | Rename to `*-application.yml` for app-of-apps discovery |\n915\t| ArgoCD App with wrong destination | Use cluster server URL, not `kubernetes.default.svc` |\n916\t| Using `fullnameOverride` in Helm apps | Don't — let the release name (`<chart>-<cluster>`) be the prefix. Overriding creates duplicates and webhook TLS mismatches |\n917\t| Using `ssd` storage class on Rackspace Spot | Use `sata` or `sata-large` instead |\n918\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"1fffcc0f-fd76-4ade-bf36-f54cf4320409","uuid":"e0dc196c-ecfe-43ac-8b37-1e2118b1654c","timestamp":"2026-07-11T12:46:29.914Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/declarative-config/k8s/CLAUDE.md","content":"# Cluster Configuration Norms\n\nThis directory contains Kubernetes manifests organized by cluster and namespace, deployed via ArgoCD GitOps.\n\n## Directory Structure\n\n```\nk8s/\n├── <cluster-name>/                    # One directory per cluster\n│   ├── <cluster>-application.yml      # ArgoCD Application for entire cluster\n│   ├── <cluster>-applicationset.yml   # ArgoCD ApplicationSet for dynamic apps\n│   └── <namespace>/                   # One directory per namespace (FLAT - no subfolders)\n│       ├── namespace.yml              # Namespace definition (required)\n│       ├── deployment.yml             # Deployment manifest\n│       ├── service.yml                # Service manifest\n│       ├── configmap.yml              # ConfigMap\n│       ├── <app>-sealedsecret.yml     # Encrypted secrets (safe for git)\n│       ├── <app>-secret.yml.template  # Secret template with per-value instructions\n│       └── ingressroute.yml           # Traefik IngressRoute\n```\n\n**IMPORTANT:** Namespace directories are FLAT - no subdirectories allowed. All manifests for a namespace go directly in the namespace folder. Use filename prefixes to organize multiple apps (e.g., `myapp-deployment.yml`, `myapp-service.yml`).\n\n## Clusters\n\n| Cluster | Purpose | Server URL |\n|---------|---------|------------|\n| `apexalgo-iad` | Production workloads (IAD region) | `https://hcp-99476ebb-4133-4a21-ac6a-6e2bdf6794c0.spot.rackspace.com` |\n| `ardenone-cluster` | Home lab / development | `https://k3s-server-a.ardenone.com:6443` |\n| `ardenone-hub` | Hub cluster (Hetzner VPS, Liqo + Garage S3) | `https://ardenone-hub.ardenone.com:6443` |\n| `ardenone-manager` | Management cluster (runs ArgoCD) | `https://kubernetes.default.svc` |\n| `rs-manager` | Rackspace Spot management cluster (IAD); runs ArgoCD for iad-* clusters | `https://kubernetes.default.svc` (in-cluster) |\n| `iad-acb` | ai-code-battle workloads (Rackspace Spot, IAD); managed by rs-manager ArgoCD | `https://hcp-ffb2da77-ad4e-4468-acfb-e1b3d477a8d7.spot.rackspace.com` |\n| `iad-ci` | CI/CD builds (Rackspace Spot, IAD); managed by rs-manager ArgoCD | `https://hcp-de5bec10-ce14-4eed-a6f4-750f3fd3a89a.spot.rackspace.com` |\n\n## File Naming Conventions\n\n### Extensions\n- Use `.yml` (preferred) or `.yaml` consistently within a namespace\n- Both are valid, but prefer `.yml` for brevity\n\n### Common File Names\n| File | Purpose |\n|------|---------|\n| `namespace.yml` | Namespace definition (REQUIRED for each namespace dir) |\n| `deployment.yml` | Deployment manifest |\n| `service.yml` | Service manifest |\n| `configmap.yml` | ConfigMap with non-sensitive config |\n| `sealedsecret.yml` | SealedSecret (encrypted, safe for git) |\n| `<app>-secret.yml.template` | Template for creating secrets (placeholders with instructions) |\n| `ingressroute.yml` | Traefik IngressRoute for external access |\n| `servicemonitor.yml` | Prometheus ServiceMonitor |\n| `hpa.yml` | HorizontalPodAutoscaler |\n| `rbac.yml` | RBAC (Role, RoleBinding, ServiceAccount) |\n| `application.yml` | ArgoCD Application for Helm charts or external sources |\n\n### Naming Patterns\n- `<app>-deployment.yml` - App-specific deployment\n- `<app>-sealedsecret.yml` - App-specific sealed secret\n- `<app>-application.yml` - ArgoCD Application for external Helm chart **(CRITICAL - see below)**\n- `*.disabled` suffix - Temporarily disabled (not applied)\n\n### CRITICAL: ArgoCD Application Naming\n\n**ArgoCD Application manifests MUST end with `-application.yml` or `-application.yaml`.**\n\nThe ArgoCD deployment uses a two-tier pattern:\n1. **ApplicationSet** syncs namespace directories (excluding `*application.yml`)\n2. **App-of-Apps** (`<cluster>-application.yml`) syncs only `*application.yml` files to ArgoCD\n\n```yaml\n# ❌ WRONG - Will NOT be deployed to ArgoCD\ncoder-helm.yml           # Not matched by app-of-apps pattern\next-postgres-operator.yml\n\n# ✅ CORRECT - Matches *application.yml pattern\ncoder-application.yml\next-postgres-operator-application.yml\nkeda-application.yml\n```\n\n**Why this matters:**\n- Files matching `*application.yml` are **excluded** from namespace sync (they don't deploy to target cluster)\n- These files are **included** by the app-of-apps Application (they deploy to ArgoCD namespace)\n- Incorrect naming means your Helm chart Application never gets created in ArgoCD\n\n## Critical Rules\n\n### 1. ALWAYS Set ImagePullSecrets for Private Images\n\n**All deployments using private Docker Hub images MUST include `imagePullSecrets`.**\n\n```yaml\n# ✅ CORRECT - ImagePullSecret configured\napiVersion: apps/v1\nkind: Deployment\nspec:\n  template:\n    spec:\n      imagePullSecrets:\n      - name: docker-hub-registry\n      containers:\n      - name: app\n        image: ronaldraygun/my-app:1.0.0\n\n# ❌ WRONG - No ImagePullSecret (will fail with ImagePullBackOff)\napiVersion: apps/v1\nkind: Deployment\nspec:\n  template:\n    spec:\n      containers:\n      - name: app\n        image: ronaldraygun/my-app:1.0.0  # Authentication required!\n```\n\n**Available secrets by namespace:**\n- `devpod`: `docker-hub-registry`, `forgejo-container-registry`\n- `mcp`: Check with `kubectl get secrets -n mcp | grep registry`\n\n**Why this is critical:**\n- The cluster uses a registry proxy (`localhost:7439`) that requires authentication\n- Without `imagePullSecrets`, private images fail with: `pull access denied, repository does not exist or may require authorization`\n- Public images (e.g., `alpine`, `nginx`) work without secrets\n\n### 2. NO Subdirectories Below Namespace Level\nNamespace directories must be FLAT. All manifests go directly in the namespace folder.\n\n```\n# ❌ Wrong - subdirectory under namespace\nk8s/apexalgo-iad/my-namespace/my-app/deployment.yml\n\n# ✅ Correct - flat structure with prefixed filenames\nk8s/apexalgo-iad/my-namespace/my-app-deployment.yml\nk8s/apexalgo-iad/my-namespace/my-app-service.yml\nk8s/apexalgo-iad/my-namespace/other-app-deployment.yml\n```\n\n### 3. NEVER Commit Plain Secrets\n```yaml\n# ❌ NEVER commit kind: Secret with real values\napiVersion: v1\nkind: Secret\nstringData:\n  API_KEY: \"real-secret-value\"  # DANGEROUS\n\n# ✅ Use SealedSecret instead\napiVersion: bitnami.com/v1alpha1\nkind: SealedSecret\nspec:\n  encryptedData:\n    API_KEY: AgBy8w...  # Encrypted, safe for git\n```\n\n#### Secret Template Format (`<app>-secret.yml.template`)\n\nEvery namespace that needs secrets MUST have a `<app>-secret.yml.template` with placeholder values. This is committed to git and serves as documentation for what secrets are required and how to obtain them.\n\n**Naming convention:** Always use the `<app>-secret.yml.template` suffix. Never use bare `secret.yml.template`.\n\nEach secret value MUST have an inline comment explaining how to find or generate it:\n\n```yaml\n---\n# My App Secret Template\n# Fill in values, seal with kubeseal, then delete this file\napiVersion: v1\nkind: Secret\nmetadata:\n  name: my-app-secret\n  namespace: my-namespace\ntype: Opaque\nstringData:\n  # API key from example.com — log in at https://example.com/account/api and copy your key\n  API_KEY: \"REPLACE_ME\"\n  # Generate with: openssl rand -base64 32\n  DB_PASSWORD: \"REPLACE_ME\"\n  # GitHub PAT with repo scope — https://github.com/settings/tokens\n  GITHUB_TOKEN: \"REPLACE_ME\"\n```\n\n#### Sealing Workflow\n\n```bash\n# 1. Copy template and fill in real values (never commit this file)\ncp my-app-secret.yml.template my-app-secret.yml\nvi my-app-secret.yml  # fill in real values\n\n# 2. Seal with kubeseal (secrets are cluster-scoped — seal per cluster)\nkubeseal --format yaml < my-app-secret.yml > my-app-sealedsecret.yml\n\n# 3. Delete the plain secret immediately\nrm my-app-secret.yml\n\n# 4. Commit only the sealed secret and template\ngit add my-app-sealedsecret.yml my-app-secret.yml.template\ngit commit -m \"feat(my-namespace): add sealed secret\"\n```\n\n**Important:** SealedSecrets are cluster-specific. A secret sealed for `apexalgo-iad` cannot be decrypted on `ardenone-cluster` and vice versa. Always seal against the target cluster.\n\n### 4. NEVER Use Job or CronJob — Use Deployments with Internal Scheduling\n\n`kind: Job` and `kind: CronJob` are **permanently banned** from this repo. This is not a preference — it is a hard rule enforced by code review.\n\n**Why:**\n- ArgoCD cannot manage Jobs properly (non-idempotent: re-sync re-creates them)\n- CronJob-spawned Job pods are **not owned by ArgoCD** — they are never pruned when the CronJob manifest is removed or disabled. Orphaned pods hold CPU and memory reservations indefinitely, silently starving other workloads. This has caused real scheduling failures in this cluster.\n- `ImagePullBackOff` Job pods have 0 restarts and never hit `backoffLimit`, so they sit forever consuming scheduler resources with no automatic cleanup.\n\n**Use a Deployment with an internal scheduling loop instead:**\n\n```yaml\n# ❌ NEVER — CronJob creates orphaned Job pods ArgoCD cannot prune\napiVersion: batch/v1\nkind: CronJob\nmetadata:\n  name: my-task\nspec:\n  schedule: \"0 * * * *\"\n  jobTemplate: ...\n\n# ❌ NEVER — same orphan problem, plus ArgoCD re-creates on every sync\napiVersion: batch/v1\nkind: Job\nmetadata:\n  name: my-task\n\n# ✅ CORRECT — long-running Deployment with internal sleep/schedule loop\napiVersion: apps/v1\nkind: Deployment\nmetadata:\n  name: my-task\nspec:\n  replicas: 1\n  template:\n    spec:\n      containers:\n      - name: my-task\n        command:\n          - sh\n          - -c\n          - |\n            while true; do\n              run-my-task\n              sleep 3600   # run hourly\n            done\n```\n\nFor more sophisticated scheduling (cron expressions, skip-if-still-running logic), implement it inside the application process itself (e.g., Python `schedule` library, Go `robfig/cron`, Rust `tokio-cron-scheduler`). The container stays running; the work happens on an internal timer.\n\n**Existing violations in this cluster:**\n- `devimprint/newsletter-generator` — CronJob left active after manifest was `.disabled`'d; its Job pod has been in ImagePullBackOff for weeks holding 250m CPU on a node. Must be manually deleted via `kubectl delete job -n devimprint --selector=job-name=...` with cluster write access.\n\n### 5. Use Traefik IngressRoute (not Ingress)\n```yaml\n# ❌ Don't use standard Ingress\napiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1\nkind: Ingress\n\n# ✅ Use Traefik IngressRoute\napiVersion: traefik.io/v1alpha1\nkind: IngressRoute\nspec:\n  entryPoints:\n    - websecure\n  routes:\n    - match: Host(`myapp.ardenone.com`)\n      kind: Rule\n      services:\n        - name: myapp\n          port: 8080\n  tls:\n    certResolver: letsencrypt\n```\n\n### 6. Domain Naming (Cloudflare Limitation)\nCloudflare doesn't support nested subdomains. Use hyphens:\n\n```yaml\n# ❌ Wrong - nested subdomain\nHost(`api.staging.ardenone.com`)\n\n# ✅ Correct - hyphenated\nHost(`api-staging.ardenone.com`)\n```\n\n### 7. Namespace Must Exist\nEvery namespace directory MUST contain a `namespace.yml`:\n\n```yaml\napiVersion: v1\nkind: Namespace\nmetadata:\n  name: my-namespace\n  labels:\n    app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: argocd\n```\n\n### 8. Use Semver Tags for `ronaldraygun/*` Images — Never `:latest`, Never Bare SHAs\n\nAll deployments referencing `ronaldraygun/*` images MUST pin to a **semver tag** (`MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH`). `:latest` and bare git SHAs are both forbidden in manifests.\n\n```yaml\n# ❌ WRONG — `:latest` silently changes what runs, breaks rollback\nimage: ronaldraygun/acb-api:latest\n\n# ❌ WRONG — SHA tags are not human-readable, don't communicate version intent,\n#            and make it impossible to tell at a glance if a cluster is up-to-date\nimage: ronaldraygun/acb-api:sha-80334c6\nimage: ronaldraygun/acb-api:a144cf75\n\n# ✅ CORRECT — semver: readable, rollback-friendly, conveys change magnitude\nimage: ronaldraygun/acb-api:1.4.2\nimage: ronaldraygun/nap-detector:0.1.6\n```\n\n**How to implement in CI WorkflowTemplates:**\n\nEvery container repo MUST have a `containers/<name>/VERSION` file containing the current semver. The CI template reads this file, pushes both `:<semver>` and `:latest`, and the manifest references the semver tag.\n\n```yaml\n# ✅ Correct CI template pattern — reads VERSION file\n- name: resolve-version\n  script:\n    command: [sh]\n    source: |\n      VERSION=$(cat containers/my-app/VERSION)\n      echo \"$VERSION\" > /tmp/version\n- name: build-container\n  inputs:\n    parameters:\n    - name: version  # passed from resolve-version step\n  container:\n    args:\n      - --destination=docker.io/ronaldraygun/my-app:{{inputs.parameters.version}}\n      - --destination=docker.io/ronaldraygun/my-app:latest\n```\n\n```yaml\n# ❌ Wrong CI template pattern — SHA tag in annotation, no VERSION file\nmetadata:\n  annotations:\n    commit_sha: abc123\nargs:\n  - --destination=docker.io/ronaldraygun/my-app:{{workflow.annotations.commit_sha}}\n```\n\n**Rules:**\n- Bump the `VERSION` file as part of the same commit that changes the code.\n- Use `PATCH` bumps for fixes, `MINOR` for new features, `MAJOR` for breaking changes.\n- The manifest in declarative-config is updated to the new semver after a successful build.\n- Public images (`alpine`, `nginx`, `postgres`) should also use pinned versions, not `:latest`.\n\n## Helm Application Conventions\n\n### Release Naming\nArgoCD Application names determine the Helm release name. All resources are prefixed with `<chart>-<cluster>`:\n\n```yaml\n# Application name: cert-manager-ardenone-cluster\n# Creates: cert-manager-ardenone-cluster, cert-manager-ardenone-cluster-webhook, etc.\n```\n\n**NEVER use `fullnameOverride` or `nameOverride`** — these break the naming convention and create duplicate resources with TLS mismatches.\n\n### Parameters vs valuesObject\n- **`parameters`** for simple top-level values: `installCRDs`, `create-namespace`\n- **`valuesObject`** for nested config: resources, replicas, webhook settings\n\n```yaml\nhelm:\n  parameters:\n    - name: installCRDs\n      value: \"true\"\n  valuesObject:\n    resources:\n      requests:\n        cpu: 50m\n        memory: 64Mi\n```\n\n### Standard syncPolicy\nAll Applications should use this baseline:\n\n```yaml\nsyncPolicy:\n  automated:\n    prune: true\n    selfHeal: true\n    allowEmpty: true\n  syncOptions:\n    - Validate=true\n    - CreateNamespace=true\n    - PrunePropagationPolicy=foreground\n    - PruneLast=true\n    - RespectIgnoreDifferences=true\n    - ServerSideApply=true\n  retry:\n    limit: 5\n    backoff:\n      duration: 5s\n      factor: 2\n      maxDuration: 3m\nrevisionHistoryLimit: 10\n```\n\n### ignoreDifferences for Tailscale-managed Services\nServices modified by the Tailscale operator need ignoreDifferences to prevent sync conflicts:\n\n```yaml\nignoreDifferences:\n  - group: \"\"\n    kind: Service\n    jsonPointers:\n      - /spec/type\n      - /spec/externalName\n      - /metadata/finalizers\n      - /status\n```\n\n## Cross-Cluster Service Exposure\n\nServices are made accessible across clusters via the **Tailscale operator**. Each cluster runs the Tailscale operator which manages proxy pods for egress (reaching external services) and ingress (exposing cluster services to the tailnet).\n\n### Exposing a cluster service to the tailnet\n\nAdd the `tailscale.com/expose` annotation to a Service. The Tailscale operator creates a proxy pod that makes the service reachable on the tailnet.\n\n```yaml\napiVersion: v1\nkind: Service\nmetadata:\n  name: my-service\n  namespace: my-namespace\n  annotations:\n    tailscale.com/expose: \"true\"\n    tailscale.com/hostname: my-service-cluster-name\nspec:\n  type: ClusterIP\n  selector:\n    app: my-service\n  ports:\n    - port: 80\n      targetPort: 8080\n```\n\nThe service becomes reachable at `my-service-cluster-name.tail1b1987.ts.net` from any node on the tailnet.\n\n### Reaching an external service from within a cluster (egress)\n\nCreate a selector-less Service with `tailscale.com/tailnet-fqdn` pointing to the target's Tailscale hostname. The operator creates a proxy pod that routes cluster traffic to the tailnet.\n\n```yaml\napiVersion: v1\nkind: Service\nmetadata:\n  name: remote-service\n  namespace: my-namespace\n  annotations:\n    tailscale.com/tailnet-fqdn: target-hostname.tail1b1987.ts.net\nspec:\n  type: ClusterIP\n  ports:\n    - port: 8200\n      targetPort: 8200\n```\n\nPods in the cluster reach the remote service at `remote-service.my-namespace.svc.cluster.local:8200`.\n\n**Example — OpenBao egress (used by apexalgo-iad and iad-ci):**\n```yaml\nannotations:\n  tailscale.com/tailnet-fqdn: traefik-ardenone-hub.tail1b1987.ts.net\n```\nOpenBao is routed through Traefik's `openbao-api` TCP entrypoint (port 8200), not exposed directly to the tailnet.\n\n### Exposing services via Traefik vpn entrypoint\n\nFor HTTP/HTTPS services, prefer Traefik IngressRoutes on the `vpn` entrypoint over direct Tailscale exposure. The Tailscale operator already exposes Traefik itself to the tailnet, so additional services routed through Traefik don't need their own Tailscale proxy.\n\n```yaml\napiVersion: traefik.io/v1alpha1\nkind: IngressRoute\nspec:\n  entryPoints:\n    - vpn\n  routes:\n    - match: Host(`my-service.ardenone.com`)\n      kind: Rule\n      services:\n        - name: my-service\n          port: 80\n  tls:\n    secretName: vpn-wildcard-tls\n```\n\n**When to use which:**\n| Method | Use case |\n|--------|----------|\n| Traefik `vpn` entrypoint | HTTP/HTTPS services already behind Traefik — avoids extra Tailscale proxies |\n| `tailscale.com/expose` | Non-HTTP services (TCP, gRPC) or services that need their own tailnet hostname |\n| `tailscale.com/tailnet-fqdn` | Reaching a service on another cluster or external tailnet node from within a cluster |\n\n### ArgoCD Read-Only API\n\nA read-only ArgoCD API proxy is available on ardenone-manager's vpn entrypoint:\n\n```bash\ncurl -sk https://argocd-ro-ardenone-manager-ts.ardenone.com:8444/api/v1/applications\n```\n\n- No authentication required — proxy injects a read-only bearer token\n- Tailscale vpn entrypoint only (not public internet)\n- Read-only: applications, projects, clusters, repositories, logs\n\n## Common Annotations\n\n### Reloader (Stakater) — Auto-restart on Secret/ConfigMap Changes\n```yaml\n# Watch all referenced secrets/configmaps automatically\nannotations:\n  reloader.stakater.com/auto: \"true\"\n\n# Or explicitly name secrets to watch\nannotations:\n  secret.reloader.stakater.com/reload: \"my-secret,other-secret\"\n```\n\n### Homepage (gethomepage.dev) — Service Discovery Dashboard\n```yaml\nannotations:\n  gethomepage.dev/enabled: \"true\"\n  gethomepage.dev/group: \"Category Name\"\n  gethomepage.dev/name: \"App Name\"\n  gethomepage.dev/description: \"Short description\"\n  gethomepage.dev/pod-selector: \"app=my-app\"\n  gethomepage.dev/icon: \"mdi-icon-name\"  # or URL\n  gethomepage.dev/href: \"https://myapp.ardenone.com\"\n```\n\n### External-DNS — Cloudflare Tunnel Routing\n```yaml\nannotations:\n  external-dns.alpha.kubernetes.io/hostname: myapp.ardenone.com\n  external-dns.alpha.kubernetes.io/target: <tunnel-id>.cfargotunnel.com\n```\n\n### ArgoCD Image Updater — Automated Image Updates\n```yaml\nannotations:\n  argocd-image-updater.argoproj.io/image-list: app=registry/image\n  argocd-image-updater.argoproj.io/app.update-strategy: semver\n  argocd-image-updater.argoproj.io/app.allow-tags: \"regexp:^(\\\\d+)\\\\.(\\\\d+)\\\\.(\\\\d+)$\"\n  argocd-image-updater.argoproj.io/write-back-method: argocd\n```\n\n## IngressRoute Patterns\n\n### Entrypoints\n\nTraefik has two ingress paths, each with its own entrypoint:\n\n| Entrypoint | Port | Source | Protocol | Use Case |\n|------------|------|--------|----------|----------|\n| `websecure` | 8443 | Cloudflare Tunnel (cloudflared) | HTTPS | Public internet access |\n| `vpn` | 8444 | Headscale mesh (tailscale) | HTTPS | Internal VPN-only access |\n\nBoth entrypoints use TLS. VPN-only services still need a TLS certificate (or use Traefik's default cert).\n\n**Choose entrypoints based on visibility:**\n\n```yaml\n# External only — accessible via Cloudflare (public internet)\nentryPoints: [websecure]\n\n# Internal only — accessible via headscale VPN mesh\nentryPoints: [vpn]\n\n# Both — accessible from internet AND VPN\nentryPoints: [websecure, vpn]\n```\n\n**Existing IngressRoutes default to `websecure` (external only).** Only add `vpn` when the service should be reachable from the headscale mesh.\n\n**Guidelines:**\n- VPN-only services (`entryPoints: [vpn]`) do NOT need external-dns annotations\n- VPN-only services MUST still create a Certificate resource and reference it via `tls.secretName`\n- VPN-only services may skip the forward-auth middleware since headscale ACLs already control access\n- Services exposed on both entrypoints can share the same TLS certificate\n\n**Example — VPN-only service:**\n```yaml\napiVersion: cert-manager.io/v1\nkind: Certificate\nmetadata:\n  name: my-internal-service-cert\n  namespace: my-namespace\nspec:\n  secretName: my-internal-service-tls\n  duration: 2160h\n  renewBefore: 360h\n  issuerRef:\n    name: letsencrypt-cloudflare\n    kind: ClusterIssuer\n  commonName: \"my-internal-service.ardenone.com\"\n  dnsNames:\n    - \"my-internal-service.ardenone.com\"\n---\napiVersion: traefik.io/v1alpha1\nkind: IngressRoute\nmetadata:\n  name: my-internal-service\n  namespace: my-namespace\nspec:\n  entryPoints:\n    - vpn\n  routes:\n    - match: Host(`my-internal-service.ardenone.com`)\n      kind: Rule\n      services:\n        - name: my-internal-service\n          port: 8080\n  tls:\n    secretName: my-internal-service-tls\n```\n\n**Example — dual access (internet + VPN):**\n```yaml\napiVersion: traefik.io/v1alpha1\nkind: IngressRoute\nmetadata:\n  name: my-service\n  namespace: my-namespace\n  annotations:\n    external-dns.alpha.kubernetes.io/hostname: my-service.ardenone.com\n    external-dns.alpha.kubernetes.io/target: <tunnel-id>.cfargotunnel.com\nspec:\n  entryPoints:\n    - websecure\n    - vpn\n  routes:\n    - match: Host(`my-service.ardenone.com`)\n      kind: Rule\n      services:\n        - name: my-service\n          port: 8080\n      middlewares:\n        - name: ardenone-com-traefik-auth\n          namespace: traefik\n  tls:\n    secretName: my-service-tls\n```\n\n### Authentication Middleware\nMost public IngressRoutes reference a shared auth middleware in the traefik namespace:\n\n```yaml\nmiddlewares:\n  - name: ardenone-com-traefik-auth\n    namespace: traefik\n```\n\n### Certificate Issuers\n- **`letsencrypt-cloudflare`** (ClusterIssuer) — for public domains via Cloudflare DNS challenge\n- **`ardenone-ca-issuer`** (ClusterIssuer) — for internal/cluster-only TLS (e.g., webhooks, internal services)\n\nStandard certificate duration: 2160h (90d), renewBefore: 360h (15d).\n\n### TLS Certificates Are Required\n\nEvery IngressRoute on both entrypoints (`websecure` and `vpn`) MUST have a per-service Certificate resource and reference it via `tls.secretName`. There is no default/wildcard fallback. Do NOT use bare `tls: {}`.\n\n```yaml\n# ✅ Correct — explicit cert\ntls:\n  secretName: my-service-tls\n\n# ❌ Wrong — no default cert configured\ntls: {}\n```\n\n## Disabled Files\n\nAppend `.disabled` to temporarily disable a manifest without deleting it:\n```\nmy-feature-deployment.yml.disabled    # Not applied by ArgoCD\n```\n\n## ArgoCD Integration\n\n### ApplicationSet (Recommended)\nEach cluster has an ApplicationSet that auto-discovers namespaces. It uses a two-tier pattern:\n\n1. **Manifest ApplicationSet** — syncs namespace directories, excludes `*application.yml`:\n   - Include: `{*.yaml,*.yml}`\n   - Exclude: `{ignore/*,*application.yml}`\n   - Auto-names apps as `{{path.basename}}-ns-<cluster>` (e.g., `devpod-ns-ardenone-cluster`)\n\n2. **App-of-Apps Application** — syncs only `*application.yml` files to ArgoCD:\n   - Include: `{*application.yaml,*application.yml}`\n   - These deploy Helm charts and external sources\n\n```yaml\n# apexalgo-iad-applicationset.yml\napiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1\nkind: ApplicationSet\nspec:\n  generators:\n  - git:\n      repoURL: https://github.com/jedarden/declarative-config.git\n      directories:\n      - path: k8s/apexalgo-iad/*\n```\n\n### Helm Chart Applications (`*application.yml`)\n\nFor Helm charts or external sources, create a file matching `*application.yml` in the cluster directory.\n**These are automatically deployed — no `kubectl apply` needed.** The cluster's app-of-apps Application\n(also in declarative-config) scans `k8s/<cluster>/` for `*application.yml` files and applies them to\nArgoCD on push. Just push to `main`.\n\n**Management chain by cluster:**\n- `k8s/ardenone-manager/*application.yml` → argocd on ardenone-manager (self-managing)\n- `k8s/rs-manager/*application.yml` → `applications-rs-manager` app on rs-manager (self-managing)\n- `k8s/iad-acb/*application.yml` → `applications-iad-acb` app on rs-manager (manages iad-acb)\n- `k8s/iad-ci/*application.yml` → rs-manager ArgoCD (manages iad-ci)\n\n```yaml\napiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1\nkind: Application\nmetadata:\n  name: my-helm-app-<cluster>\n  namespace: argocd\nspec:\n  source:\n    repoURL: https://charts.example.com\n    chart: my-chart\n    targetRevision: 1.0.0\n  destination:\n    server: https://<cluster-api-server>\n    namespace: my-namespace\n```\n\n## Adding a New Workload\n\n1. **Create namespace directory:**\n   ```bash\n   mkdir -p k8s/<cluster>/<namespace>\n   ```\n\n2. **Add namespace.yml:**\n   ```yaml\n   apiVersion: v1\n   kind: Namespace\n   metadata:\n     name: <namespace>\n   ```\n\n3. **Add deployment and service:**\n   ```bash\n   # deployment.yml, service.yml, configmap.yml as needed\n   ```\n\n4. **Add secrets (if needed):**\n   ```bash\n   # Create template\n   cp secret.yml.template <app>-secret.yml\n   # Fill in values, seal, delete original\n   kubeseal --format yaml < <app>-secret.yml > <app>-sealedsecret.yml\n   rm <app>-secret.yml\n   ```\n\n5. **Commit and push:**\n   ```bash\n   git add k8s/<cluster>/<namespace>/\n   git commit -m \"feat(<namespace>): add deployment manifests\"\n   git push\n   ```\n\n6. **ArgoCD auto-syncs** - ApplicationSet picks up new namespace\n\n7. **Monitor the sync** - After pushing, verify ArgoCD syncs successfully:\n   ```bash\n   # Via ArgoCD API (read-only token)\n   curl -sk \"https://argocd-manager.ardenone.com/api/v1/applications/<app-name>\" \\\n     -H \"Authorization: Bearer $ARGOCD_TOKEN\" | python3 -c \"\n   import json, sys; data = json.load(sys.stdin)\n   s = data['status']\n   print(f'sync={s[\\\"sync\\\"][\\\"status\\\"]} health={s[\\\"health\\\"][\\\"status\\\"]}')\n   for c in s.get('conditions', []):\n       print(f'  {c[\\\"type\\\"]}: {c[\\\"message\\\"][:120]}')\n   \"\n\n   # Via kubectl (if proxy is available)\n   kubectl --server=http://kubectl-ardenone-cluster-1:8001 get pods -n <namespace>\n   ```\n   Watch for: CRD validation errors, webhook TLS mismatches, duplicate resource conflicts, and deployment progress deadline failures. Do not consider the task complete until the ArgoCD Application shows **Synced** and **Healthy**.\n\n## Debugging\n\n```bash\n# Check ArgoCD sync status\nkubectl get applications -n argocd | grep <namespace>\n\n# View sync details\nkubectl describe application <app-name> -n argocd\n\n# Force sync\nargocd app sync <app-name>\n\n# Check pod status\nkubectl get pods -n <namespace>\nkubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n <namespace>\nkubectl logs <pod-name> -n <namespace>\n```\n\n## CI/CD — Checking Argo Workflow Logs\n\nAll CI runs on Argo Workflows in `iad-ci`. Workflow templates live in `k8s/iad-ci/argo-workflows/`.\n\n```bash\n# List recent workflow runs and their status\nkubectl --kubeconfig=/home/coding/.kube/iad-ci.kubeconfig \\\n  get workflows -n argo-workflows --sort-by=.metadata.creationTimestamp | tail -20\n\n# Get a specific workflow's result and error message\nkubectl --kubeconfig=/home/coding/.kube/iad-ci.kubeconfig \\\n  get workflow <workflow-name> -n argo-workflows \\\n  -o jsonpath='{.status.phase} - {.status.message}'\n\n# Get per-step failure details (most useful for debugging)\nkubectl --kubeconfig=/home/coding/.kube/iad-ci.kubeconfig \\\n  get workflow <workflow-name> -n argo-workflows -o json | python3 -c \"\nimport json,sys\nw = json.load(sys.stdin)\nfor node in w['status'].get('nodes',{}).values():\n    if node.get('phase') in ('Failed','Error'):\n        print(node['displayName'], '-', node['phase'])\n        print('  msg:', node.get('message',''))\n        for p in node.get('inputs',{}).get('parameters',[]):\n            print(f'  input {p[\\\"name\\\"]}={p.get(\\\"value\\\")}')\n\"\n\n# Stream logs from a running step pod\nkubectl --kubeconfig=/home/coding/.kube/iad-ci.kubeconfig \\\n  get pods -n argo-workflows -l workflows.argoproj.io/workflow=<workflow-name>\nkubectl --kubeconfig=/home/coding/.kube/iad-ci.kubeconfig \\\n  logs -n argo-workflows <pod-name> -c main -f\n```\n\n> **Critical:** `podGC: OnPodCompletion` deletes pods immediately after they finish — you cannot retrieve logs after the fact via kubectl. Either stream logs while the pod is running, or use the Argo UI at `https://argo-ci.ardenone.com` (logs are kept for 30min on success, 2h on failure).\n\n### Submitting a build manually\n\n```bash\nkubectl --kubeconfig=/home/coding/.kube/iad-ci.kubeconfig create -f - <<EOF\napiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1\nkind: Workflow\nmetadata:\n  generateName: <template-name>-manual-\n  namespace: argo-workflows\nspec:\n  workflowTemplateRef:\n    name: <template-name>\nEOF\n```\n\n## Rackspace Spot Storage Classes\n\nAll Rackspace Spot clusters (iad-ci, iad-native-ads, iad-options, iad-kalshi) use OpenStack Cinder CSI. The correct storage classes are:\n\n| Class | Use case |\n|-------|----------|\n| `sata` | Default — general workloads, databases, queues |\n| `sata-large` | Large volumes (>100Gi) |\n| `ssd` | **Do not use** — reserved/default on some clusters but avoid |\n| `ssd-large` | **Do not use** |\n\n**Always use `sata` or `sata-large` on Rackspace Spot clusters.** Never use `ssd`, `local-path`, or omit `storageClassName` (the default varies by cluster).\n\n## Rackspace Spot Node Pools\n\nNode pools are configured in the Rackspace Spot UI — they are **not** managed through declarative-config.\n\n**Always use spot node pools. Never create or keep on-demand node pools.** On-demand nodes on Rackspace Spot cost significantly more than spot and defeat the purpose of using Spot clusters. If an on-demand node pool exists, delete it from the Spot UI immediately.\n\nTo identify node types on a running cluster:\n```bash\nkubectl get nodes -o json | python3 -c \"\nimport json,sys\nfor n in json.load(sys.stdin)['items']:\n    name = n['metadata']['name']\n    ntype = n['metadata']['labels'].get('servers.ngpc.rxt.io/type', '?')\n    pool = n['metadata']['labels'].get('nodepool.ngpc.rxt.io/name', '?')\n    print(f'{name}: type={ntype} pool={pool}')\n\"\n```\n\nAny node showing `type=ondemand` must be investigated — its node pool should be deleted from the Spot UI.\n\n## Common Mistakes\n\n| Mistake | Fix |\n|---------|-----|\n| Subdirectory under namespace | Flatten - use filename prefixes instead |\n| Committing plain Secret | Use SealedSecret |\n| Using `kind: Job` or `kind: CronJob` | **Hard ban** — use Deployment with internal scheduling loop; see Rule #4 |\n| Missing namespace.yml | Add namespace definition |\n| Nested subdomain | Use hyphenated subdomain |\n| Using Ingress | Use Traefik IngressRoute |\n| Using `:latest` for `ronaldraygun/*` images | Pin to an immutable SHA or semver tag |\n| Missing resource limits | Add requests/limits |\n| ArgoCD App named `*-helm.yml` | Rename to `*-application.yml` for app-of-apps discovery |\n| ArgoCD App with wrong destination | Use cluster server URL, not `kubernetes.default.svc` |\n| Using `fullnameOverride` in Helm apps | Don't — let the release name (`<chart>-<cluster>`) be the prefix. Overriding creates duplicates and webhook TLS mismatches |\n| Using `ssd` storage class on Rackspace Spot | Use `sata` or `sata-large` instead |\n","numLines":918,"startLine":1,"totalLines":918}}}
